LAW AND MOTION 302, DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CLARIFY THE STATUTORY BASIS FOR THE CLAIM, E.G., GOV'T 815.2. PLAINTIFF ALSO HAS LEAVE TO MORE CLEARLY ALLEGE ULTIMATE FACTS REGARDING WHY THE JOINT CONFERENCE WAS NEGLIGENT IN THIS PARTICULAR INSTANCE. THE COURT REJECTS DEFENDANT'S RELIANCE ON THE GOVERNMENTAL IMMUNITIES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489; DEPT. 302. =302/EHG

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO NOVEMBER 10, 2015, AT 9:30 A.M., IN DEPT. 302, TO GIVE PLAINTIFF THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. PLAINTIFF MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN NOVEMBER 3, 2015 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE DENIED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). JUDGE ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK FELICIA GREEN; DEPT. 302.

calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-17-2017 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Feb-02-2017 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-02-17 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Seperate Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Declaration Of Nancy A. Mcpherson; Memorandum Of Points And Authorites; (Proposed) Order
Off Calendar Dec-16-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant San Francisco Unified School District's motion for summary judgment is denied. The District failed to satisfy its initial summary judgment burden. In a case of this nature the critical issue is foreseeability of the alleged criminal attack on plaintiff Ebony Isler. Ms. Isler's complaint alleges in paragraph 4 of her first amended complaint that the District was "on notice that Ms. Isler's assailants posed a serious threat to her safety." Where a party has notice that a person's safety is at risk from possible criminal behavior and can take minimal steps to avoid that risk, generally the party has a duty to take the minimal steps to avoid that risk. Yet the District's separate statement, which is the sole source of facts for which it can seek summary judgment, presents no facts on the issues of alleged notice or alleged steps that could have been taken to avoid the alleged risk. Even if the District satisfied its summary judgment burden, Ms. Isler has presented sufficient facts to create triable issues regarding the notice and steps that could have been taken to avoid the risk. (Plaintiff's Undisputed Material Facts 9-15). Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CCP 437 c(G) and CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-30-2015 Set For Trial On Aug-15-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. DANIEL R. STERRETT, ESQ. of Sterrett Law, Tel No. 415-829-4485 appearing for for Plaintiff;
Atty. KEVIN E. GILBERT, ESQ. of Lozano Smith, Tel No. 925-953-1620 appearing for Defendant San Francisco Unified School District.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. Cal. Gov't Code sec. 815.2 serves as a statutory basis for liability. The amended complaint pleads sufficient facts regarding liability. Plaintiff identifies the principal as having knowledge of the danger, but still deciding to have the joint meeting. Plaintiff also alleges the basis for the principal's knowledge. Plaintiff correctly cites Delgado v. Trax (2005) 36 Cal.4th 224. There is a sliding scale analysis between foreseeability and the burden imposed on the defendant when determining a duty. Here, plaintiff seeks to impose a minimal duty (separate conferences) and therefore, heightened foreseeability is not necessary. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to amend to clarify the statutory basis for the claim, e.g., Gov't 815.2.
